surfaces.evidence: SannySoft local dogfood observed Chrome UA through installed Google Chrome; no target persona coherence score was performed.
No BrowserLeaks/Pixelscan client-hints run was executed.
SannySoft local dogfood observed platform MacIntel through installed Chrome; no target persona coherence score was performed.
No timezone/proxy-country coherence detector row was completed.
Local dogfood captured zh-TW language; cross-surface proxy/timezone coherence was not scored.
No BrowserLeaks/CreepJS screen/window/DPR row was completed.
No detector row captured hardwareConcurrency/deviceMemory coherence.
No BrowserLeaks/CreepJS canvas row was completed.
Local SannySoft dogfood captured host Apple/ANGLE WebGL renderer; no target persona comparison was performed.
No BrowserLeaks/CreepJS/BrowserScan audio row was completed.
No BrowserLeaks/CreepJS/Pixelscan font row was completed.
No BrowserLeaks/WebRTC or proxy-leak detector row was completed.
SannySoft local dogfood page exposed permissions prompt state in raw DOM summary, but no full CreepJS permissions row was completed.
No BrowserScan storage/incognito row was completed.
SannySoft local dogfood direct-host run reported WebDriver present and navigator.webdriver=true.
No proxy detector run was executed.
BrowseForge dogfood used distinct profile IDs and isolated same-origin localStorage across two sessions.
Only macOS arm64 local Chrome dogfood was exercised; no Linux/Windows detector evidence exists.

decision: Keep live-detector-evidence release gate blocked. Dogfood proved BrowseForge wiring and exposed automation detection; it did not prove anti-detect readiness.
